title: Login form submitted with whitespace-only credentials does not authenticate and shows field-level validation errors
description: Verifies that submitting the login form with only spaces, tabs, or newlines in the email/username and password fields is treated as empty input — not as valid credentials — and that the backend is never called. A common production bug is that whitespace passes client-side 'required' checks, reaches the server, and either throws an unhandled 500 or — worse — matches a stored hash for a whitespace password.
expected_result: PASS: The form is blocked client-side; both fields show validation errors; the URL stays at /login; no network request is made to the authentication API. FAIL: The form submits, a network request fires (even if it returns 401), the user sees a generic 'Invalid credentials' error instead of a field-level 'required' error, or — critically — the user is authenticated.

test_steps.expected: Login page loads with email/username and password fields visible

test_steps.action: Fill email/username field with whitespace-only content (three spaces)
test_steps.expected: Field visually appears filled but contains only whitespace

test_steps.action: Fill password field with whitespace-only content (two tabs)
test_steps.expected: Password field visually appears filled but contains only whitespace

test_steps.action: Click the submit / Sign In button
test_steps.expected: Form submission is blocked; validation error messages appear on both fields

test_steps.action: Verify email/username validation error message is visible
test_steps.expected: An inline error such as 'Email or username is required' is displayed beneath the email field

test_steps.action: Verify password validation error message is visible
test_steps.expected: An inline error such as 'Password is required' is displayed beneath the password field

test_steps.action: Verify the user has NOT been redirected away from the login page
test_steps.expected: URL remains /login; no dashboard or home page is shown
